name: Land of Sacred Stones
recruits: Eirika — Automatically, start
Seth — Automatically, start
warns: Tutorial map — Seth handles everything; keep Eirika safe

name: Escape!
recruits: Franz — Automatically, start
warns: Use Seth aggressively to clear enemies and escort Eirika to the gate

name: The Protected
recruits: Gilliam — Joins from the west as an ally NPC
Vanessa — Peg. Knight; arrives as reinforcement from the east
Moulder — NPC ally; joins automatically after surviving
warns: Vanessa has flying utility — deploy her in every chapter from here on

name: The Bandits of Borgo
recruits: Ross — Visit the house with Eirika; he and Garcia join together
Garcia — Joins with Ross; strong Fighter with good growths
Neimi — Visit the eastern house
Colm — Enemy Thief; have Neimi (or Eirika) talk to him before he moves away
warns: Do not kill Colm — he is your only early thief
Ross is a Journeyman trainee — give him early kills; his double-promotion at level 10 is worth the investment

name: Ancient Horrors
recruits: Artur — NPC ally Monk; arrives from the north
Lute — Visit the village; she joins automatically
warns: Monsters (Revenants) are introduced here; magic and critical hits are effective
Lute has the highest magic growth in the game but needs EXP to get going

name: The Empire's Reach
recruits: Natasha — NPC ally Cleric; rescue her and keep her alive
Joshua — Enemy Myrmidon; NATASHA must talk to him (he is a hired guard who can be persuaded)
warns: CRITICAL: Only Natasha can recruit Joshua — position her to intercept him before he closes in on enemies
Do not kill Joshua; he becomes one of the best myrmidons in the game

name: Unbroken Heart
warns: Mandatory interlude — you control Ephraim, Kyle, and Forde; they officially join your army in Ch. 8
Items collected here carry over to the main game
Orson appears here as an ally; he later betrays you — story context only

name: Victims of War
items: Chest contents — bring Colm for the locked chests

name: Waterside Renvall
recruits: Tana — Frelian Peg. Knight; rescued from Renvall; joins after the chapter
steal: Energy Ring (Mage at center — high-value stat booster, raises Str/Mag)
warns: Tana is needed to recruit Cormag on Ephraim's route later — keep her trained

name: It's a Trap!
recruits: Ephraim — Story join; Eirika and Ephraim reunite
Kyle — Story join with Ephraim (Cavalier, balanced growths)
Forde — Story join with Ephraim (Cavalier, speed-focused)
warns: Ephraim's party bursts in as the chapter resolves — no action needed to recruit them
Choose your route when prompted after the chapter ends

title: Save before Ch. 8 — route split at end of this chapter
body: After Ch. 8 you permanently choose Eirika's route or Ephraim's route for this save file (Ch. 9–14 differ completely). Ephraim's route is recommended for first-time players — it is the only route with Tethys, the game's sole dancer. Duplicate your save here to experience both routes. The Tower of Valni also opens on the world map around this point — use it to grind skirmishes and levels at any time.

title: Route split — Eirika's route vs. Ephraim's route

opts.label: Eirika's Route
opts.note: Follows Eirika through Jehanna. Gerik + Marisa are strong. No dancer available on this route.

opts.label: Ephraim's Route
opts.units: Duessel (Great Knight) · Cormag (Wyvern) · Tethys (Dancer) · Ewan (Trainee) — Ephraim route exclusive
opts.note: Follows Ephraim assaulting Grado. Tethys is irreplaceable dancer utility. Strongly recommended for first run.

excl: Duessel / Cormag / Tethys / Ewan — Ephraim route only

name: Distant Blade
recruits: Tana — Joins as NPC/story ally (if not already recruited)
Gerik — NPC mercenary; joins automatically as ally
Marisa — Enemy Myrmidon; Gerik must talk to her (she is a Grado mercenary, Gerik's commander)
warns: Position Gerik near Marisa quickly — she moves and will advance into your lines
Do not kill Marisa; she is an excellent Swordmaster

name: Fort Rigwald
recruits: Duessel — Story join; the Obsidian, a veteran Grado general who refuses to fight his old allies
Tana — Joins as NPC/story ally (escaped to find Ephraim)
warns: Duessel has excellent base stats as a Great Knight — deploy him immediately
Keep Tana alive and trained; she is required to recruit Cormag in Ch. 10

name: Revolt at Carcino
recruits: Innes — Story join; Frelian Prince arrives with reinforcements
warns: Innes is a Ranger (promotes to Sniper or Ranger); his high Skill and Spd make him reliable

name: Turning Traitor
recruits: Cormag — Enemy Wyvern Rider; TANA must talk to him (his brother Glen was killed on enemy orders and he seeks new purpose)
warns: Only Tana can recruit Cormag — have her intercept him before he engages your units
Do not kill Cormag; he is a solid Wyvern Knight

name: Creeping Darkness
recruits: Amelia — Enemy Soldier (green-armored); have Eirika or Franz talk to her (she joined Grado out of necessity and can be convinced to defect)
warns: Amelia is a Recruit trainee — she double-promotes at level 10; worthwhile investment if started here

name: Phantom Ship
warns: Ship map — terrain limits movement; position your units carefully to avoid being surrounded
Pirate-class enemies are common here; watch Eirika's and mage units' positioning

name: Village of Silence
recruits: L'Arachel — Flamboyant Troubadour; arrives as NPC; joins with Dozla after the chapter
Dozla — Joins with L'Arachel (Berserker; joins too late to be useful in combat)
Rennac — Rogue; L'Arachel can recruit him for free, otherwise Eirika must pay 10,000G
items: Master Seal (chest — universal promotion item, use on anyone)
warns: Have L'Arachel talk to Rennac to avoid paying the 10,000G fee
Rennac is a Rogue (opens doors/chests without keys) — keep Colm or Rennac on your team for loot

name: Landing at Taizel
recruits: Tethys — Dancer; joins as NPC ally (ONLY dancer in FE8 — protect her at all costs)
Ewan — Pupil trainee; visit the house (Tethys's companion and student)
Amelia — Enemy Soldier; Ephraim or Duessel talks to her
warns: TETHYS IS IRREPLACEABLE — she is the only dancer in the entire game; never put her in danger
Ewan is a Pupil trainee who can become a Summoner (unique class that summons Phantom units) via Shaman promotion path

name: Hamill Canyon
recruits: Gerik — If not recruited in Ch. 9, he joins here as an NPC ally

name: Fluorspar's Oath
recruits: L'Arachel — Joins here on Ephraim's route (with Dozla and Rennac; same recruit conditions apply)
warns: L'Arachel recruit note: have her talk to Rennac to avoid the 10,000G fee

name: Queen of White Dunes
items: Warp (hidden — check desert tile; move mounted/flying units over sand to search)
Excalibur — Sacred Twin wind tome (Eirika route)
warns: Desert map — search sand tiles for hidden items including Warp
Final Eirika-route chapter; routes converge at Ch. 15

name: Father and Son
items: Gleipnir — Sacred Twin dark tome (Ephraim route)
warns: Final Ephraim-route chapter; routes converge at Ch. 15

name: Scorched Sand
recruits: Knoll — Enemy Shaman held in the prison; have Eirika or Ephraim talk to him to recruit
steal: Guiding Ring (Shaman with Eclipse — promotes mage-type units)
warns: Knoll can become a Summoner — a unique class that summons Phantom units to block passages; niche but fun
Both routes converge here — all of your route-specific characters carry forward

name: Ruled by Madness
recruits: Saleh — Story join; Sage from Caer Pelyn with excellent base magic
Myrrh — Story join; Great Dragon Manakete with a powerful but limited Dragonstone
steal: Red Gem (Sage — Eirika side; or Great Knight reinforcement — Ephraim side)
warns: Myrrh's Dragonstone has limited uses — do not waste it on weak enemies
Use a Hammerne staff on Myrrh's stone when it wears low

name: River of Regrets
warns: Prepare your team — the game ramps up from here
Stock healing staves and stat-booster items before advancing

name: Two Faces of Evil
warns: Two back-to-back maps (Eirika and Ephraim split up then rejoin) — ensure both halves of your team are stocked with healing

name: Last Hope
items: Garm — legendary axe; dropped by the boss
steal: Goddess Icon (Archer to the north — raises Luck)
warns: Boss carries Garm (Sacred Twins weapon) — kill him to obtain it
Prepare for the final stretch; use remaining stat boosters now

name: Darkling Woods
warns: Dense forest terrain — flying and mounted units are slowed; adjust formation
Lyon is involved in this chapter; a critical story beat before the finale

name: The Last Stand
warns: Use all remaining stat boosters before the Final chapter
Eirika wields Sieglinde, Ephraim wields Siegmund — both received via story events before the finale

name: Sacred Stones
warns: Fomortiis (the Demon King) is the final boss — use the Sacred Twins weapons for best damage
Deploy both Eirika and Ephraim; Myrrh and Seth are also excellent here
No chapter after this — use everything
